summary: Benchmark scope is the macro loop
context: Committed benchmark scope as multi-sprint/multi-release macro-loop coordination benchmark; 892-session results are pilot evidence, the contribution is the macro-loop methodology.
rationale: evaluation across multiple sprints and releases on a sustained codebase that accumulates decisions, debt, and architectural constraints. The unit of evaluation is the codebase over time, not single sessions or single sprints. Agent Teams is reframed
constraints: Must distinguish from Anthropic Agent Teams positioning
Must frame around production-quality sustained software rather than greenfield POCs
Must preserve current pilot data as supporting evidence without overclaiming

alternatives.option: time, not single sessions or single sprints. Agent Teams is reframed as a tool inside the inner loop (orthogonal to substrate choice), not a condition to test against. Current 892-session results are directional pilot evidence; the contribution is the macro-loop evaluation methodology, which does not currently exist in the field
greenfield POC. Rejected alternatives: (1) Exploration-efficiency ROI as primary wedge

single sessions or single sprints. Agent Teams is reframed as a tool inside the inner loop (orthogonal to substrate choice), not a condition to test against. Current 892-session results are directional pilot evidence; the contribution is the macro-loop evaluation methodology, which does not currently exist in the field

assumptions: Anthropic Agent Teams (Feb 2026) targets the inner coordination loop, not cross-sprint sustained-codebase evolution
Existing benchmarks (LongMemEval, LoCoMo, tau-bench, CooperBench, REP) do not measure multi-sprint/multi-release coordination on a sustained codebase
892-session pilot is directional not confirmatory — sample sizes and scenario mix were chosen for single-sprint signal, not macro-loop validation
